description: Known agents in the psychology-agent mesh. BFT-inspired architecture: peer agents function as replicas (consensus participants); human and public function as clients (submitters). /sync reads this to determine who to check for activity and who to consider for outbound messages.

psy-session.description: Human-agent dyadic cognitive system — interactive Claude Code session coupling human operator with psychology-agent. Privileged client: directs work, configures replicas, reviews deliverables.
psy-session.notes: Renamed from 'human' (Session 89). The dyad (LLM-factors psychology) represents the coupled human+agent cognitive system during interactive sessions. Autonomous psychology-agent operates independently on chromabook.

safety-quotient-agent.description: PSQ agent — psychometric model training, calibration, structural analysis (PSQ scoring)
safety-quotient-agent.notes: PSQ agent. Identity: safety-quotient-agent (formerly psq-sub-agent — legacy prefix retained for historical transport files). Cross-repo transport via git remote fetch. Always considered for outbound — any psychometric, scoring, or model-related content SHOULD be surfaced.

unratified-agent.description: Blog platform operator, consumer of PSQ scores, content publisher — operates safety-quotient-lab/unratified
unratified-agent.notes: Peer agent. Cross-repo transport via git remote fetch. Blog posts, content quality, PSQ display, persuasion audits — content-facing topics SHOULD be surfaced.

observatory-agent.description: Data observatory — psychoemotional safety research data, ICESCR framing, methodology validation
observatory-agent.notes: Peer agent. Cross-repo transport via git remote fetch. Research data, methodology, ICESCR framing. 10 transport sessions. Communicates with unratified-agent directly; messages to psychology-agent typically route through shared sessions (psq-scoring, dignity-instrument).

operations-agent.description: Operations agent — mesh operations, autonomous sync, circuit breaker, infrastructure management
operations-agent.notes: Peer agent. Bootstrapped Session 83. Runs on chromabook:8081 (meshd). Owns mesh operations domain (circuit breaker, autonomous sync scheduling). Transport via branches + operator-mediated file exchange.

claude-control.description: Systems automation agent — cross-project cogarch learnings, infrastructure patterns
claude-control.notes: Infrequent contact. Cross-project findings exchange. Active in operations-agent standup (turns 1,4,6).

outbound_routing.description: When /sync detects content relevant to a registered agent, it SHOULD draft an outbound message or flag for user review. Routing rules by content domain:

rules.action: draft outbound if safety-quotient-agent has active session; flag if not
flag for user review — substance decision
notify if cogarch mirror directive active
notify if infrastructure changes affect shared resources
flag for user review — substance decision
broadcast to all peers — consensus domain
